quality_notes: Add vulnerability: mention a mistake, failure, or lesson learned. Try 'I was wrong', 'took me years to learn', or 'finally understood'.
Strengthen CTA: replace generic 'what do you think?' with concrete question like 'what part of this does your team still get wrong?'
Add more relevant emojis to improve scanability and engagement (target: 4 to 10).
Align the post more closely to the planned diagram labels so the image and text tell the same story.
Include one fenced visual block that matches the planned diagram type.
Keep the post in the LinkedIn sweet spot: roughly 140 to 340 words.

post_text:
I clicked on an AI assistant's suggestion for a book last weekend. The assistant's recommendation was surprisingly accurate, which made me curious - I searched my own name in the AI database. What I found was uncomfortable: my professional presence was largely defined by outdated profiles and incomplete information.

Most engineers assume that online presence doesn't affect their professional discovery, but that's incorrect - public signals shape how others find and perceive us.

This realization changed my approach to online presence. 💬 How do you ensure your professional online presence accurately reflects your skills and experience? #ProfessionalDiscovery #OnlinePresence #CareerDevelopment #PersonalBrand #AIassistants

  .fi{animation:fd .5s ease-out both}

  .pu{animation:pu 2.2s ease-in-out infinite}
